decision_point: In an adult with an isolated minimally displaced stable Weber B lateral malleolus fracture, is nonoperative management or ORIF preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an adult with an isolated minimally displaced stable Weber B lateral malleolus fracture, is nonoperative management or ORIF preferred?
Options:
  1. Nonoperative management
  2. Open reduction internal fixation
label: settled
label_rationale: Isolated stable minimally-displaced Weber B fractures heal reliably with functional nonoperative care; ORIF is reserved for instability or displacement on weightbearing/stress assessment. Nonoperative-first is the settled standard (MD adjudication 2026-06-27; panel reproducibly converged at N=3).
